questions.question: Describe the main difference between nominal and ordinal categorical data. Provide an example of each type.
questions.hint: Focus on the characteristics and applications of each type

questions.question: A dataset contains two categorical variables: 'color' and 'shape'. Assuming the variables are independent, calculate the probability of a randomly selected object being red and circular. Use the following data: color (blue, red, green) = 40, red = 30, green = 30, shape (circle, square) = 50, circle = 40, square = 10. Express your answer as a fraction in simplest form.
questions.hint: Use the data to find the marginal probabilities and then apply the formula for joint probability

questions.question: What is the purpose of one-hot encoding when dealing with categorical data? Provide an example of how to perform one-hot encoding on a categorical variable.
questions.hint: Focus on the benefits and process of one-hot encoding
